summary: Epic tracking issue for enabling nullable reference type annotations across all SkiaSharp packages; 19 sub-issues (#2057–#2075) remain open as of triage.

reproEvidence.environmentDetails: Created 2022-05-22 by maintainer mattleibow. No specific runtime version.

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The csproj has <Nullable>enable</Nullable> globally but most binding files use #nullable disable; work is still in progress.

analysis.summary: This is a maintainer-created epic to track enabling C# nullable reference type annotations across all SkiaSharp NuGet packages. The global project setting enables nullable warnings but individual source files currently suppress them with #nullable disable. Only SKCanvas.cs has partial #nullable enable sections. All 19 sub-tracking issues (#2057–#2075) are still open, indicating the bulk of the work is not yet completed.
analysis.rationale: Epic/tracker issue with sub-issues per package. Classified as type/enhancement because it improves the existing API surface (adds nullability contracts without adding new functionality). Area is area/SkiaSharp as the primary package being tracked, though the work spans multiple packages. tenet/compatibility applies because nullable annotations change how consuming code interacts with the API and can surface breaking annotation changes in downstream projects.

codeInvestigation.finding: <Nullable>enable</Nullable> is set globally, meaning all files opt in to warnings by default, but most individual source files override this with #nullable disable at the top.
Contains multiple #nullable enable / #nullable disable blocks, indicating partial nullable annotation work has already been done in the main canvas class.

keySignals.text: This is an epic to track adding nullable annotations to SkiaSharp.
keySignals.interpretation: Maintainer-created tracker; not a bug report or user question.
No sub-issues have been completed; the work is still pending.

resolution.hypothesis: The work is well-scoped via sub-issues. Each sub-issue covers one NuGet package. Progress requires incrementally removing #nullable disable from each package's source files and fixing the resulting warnings.

proposals.title: Process sub-issues incrementally
proposals.description: Work through sub-issues #2057–#2075 one package at a time: remove #nullable disable from each file, fix warnings, and close the corresponding sub-issue.

resolution.recommendedReason: Sub-issue breakdown already exists; incremental approach minimizes risk of breaking consuming code.

actionability.reason: Active long-term epic with all 19 sub-issues still open. No action to close or reassign; track progress through sub-issues.

actions.description: Apply enhancement and compatibility tenet labels
